steps.title: Identify what the design isolates
steps.content: The study presents each dog in both iris-color versions across participants, but no participant sees both versions of the same dog. This setup allows the researchers to compare how the same dog is rated when it has light irises versus when it has dark irises, with only that variable changing.

steps.title: Match the control to the ruled-out alternative
steps.content:
By comparing the same dog in both iris conditions, the design rules out the possibility that the preference reflects other facial features that happen to coincide with dark irises in these specific dogs—such as certain dogs naturally having broader faces or different expressions alongside dark eyes.

A) concerns in-person versus image viewing, C) mischaracterizes what the rating task measures, and D) describes preexisting bias, none of which this design targets.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
